channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: PRAGMATA: The Hacking Reset Secret Technique to Improve Combat
item.content:encoded:
Hacking is a core component of the PRAGMATA combat mechanics and the most difficult to master. As you juggle between hacking and shooting enemies, you may find hacking cumbersome and distracting, but there is a hidden input trick that completely eliminates hacking downtime. Mastering this "Hacking Reset" tech not only allows you to hack more consistently but also lets you build the Hacking Gauge faster to access powerful abilities more often.

Table of contents

  • Why Your Hacking Might Feel Slow
  • How to Perform the Hacking Reset Tech
  • Why You Need This: Faster Overdrive and Auto-Hacking

Why Your Hacking Might Feel Slow

A common source of frustration in the first few hours of the game is how hacking and shooting don't seem to mesh well. Most players naturally hold down the L2/LT/ZL (Aim) button during combat encounters, letting it go only when they require faster mobility. However, holding the aim button prevents the Hacking Matrix from reappearing automatically.

As such, by holding your aim throughout a fight, you are actually limiting your most valuable combat asset, making combat feel much slower and clunkier than it is.

How to Perform the Hacking Reset Tech

The key to taking your PRAGMATA gameplay to the next level is treating the L2/LT/ZL button like a rhythmic pump rather than a static toggle. This Reset Trick bypasses downtime, enabling fast hacking cycles.

How to execute the Hacking Reset:

  1. Hold L2/LT to bring up the Matrix and complete your hack.
  2. Release L2/LT immediately upon a successful hack
  3. Instantly hold L2/LT again during the slowed time frame following a successful hack to force the Hacking Matrix to pop up again with no delay.

By pumping the trigger this way, you remove any frustration from the combat loop. This allows you to stack status effects and hacking damage on enemies in a fraction of the time it takes using the standard method.

While the damage difference between this tech and traditional weakpoint shooting isn't massive without builds that prioritize Hacking damage, the true value lies in simplifying your mental stack. By focusing entirely on the hacking rhythm, you remove the need to precisely aim at weakpoints (which are sometimes placed on enemies' sides or backs), allowing you to manage crowded encounters with much less stress.

Why You Need This: Faster Overdrive and Auto-Hacking

Mastering this Hacking Reset tech comes with some additional benefits that will further improve your gameplay.

  • Since you are completing multiple hacks in a short time, your Hacking Gauge will fill up significantly faster. This lets you trigger Overdrive Mode way more often
  • As it fills the Hacking Gauge much faster, this Reset Trick also enables more uses of Auto-Hacking, which requires a portion of the gauge to execute.
  • When Offense Mode is active, hacks deal increased damage to enemies that have been opened. The Hacking Reset Tech turns your hacking into a weapon with a high rate of fire that can melt health bars without using physical ammo.
  • As you are using Hacking as your primary damage dealer, you also have more chances to activate Critical Shot nodes for increased Lunafilament gain.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-the-hacking-reset-secret-technique-to-optimize-combat-flow/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: All Mr. Cabin Stamp Rewards – Spend Your Cabin Coins Wisely
item.content:encoded:
Unlike some of the other currencies, Cabin Coins in PRAGMATA are a limited resource. You will only find these by thoroughly scouring sectors or mastering the optional objectives in Training Simulations like Bad Blood or Floor It.

Because you never find these coins in droves, you cannot afford to spend them blindly. The Mr. Cabin Stamp Card hides many of its rewards, and while some unlock vital gameplay enhancements, others are purely cosmetic 3D models. To help you avoid wasting your rare coins, we have listed all rewards below.

NOTE: The guide currently lists all the rewards of the Trainee and Associate Card. The rewards for other stamp cards will be added in a future update.

Table of contents

  • How the Mr. Cabin Stamp System Works
  • Every Mr. Cabin Stamp Reward
    • Trainee Card
    • Associate Card

How the Mr. Cabin Stamp System Works

Once you find a Cabin Coin, you can stamp one of the cards by speaking with Mr. Cabin in the Shelter. The rewards are laid out in a grid, and most are hidden behind "???" icons until you spend a coin to reveal the reward.

This is where the risk lies. If you don't engage with the side content, you could easily waste your limited coins on a bot model instead of a critical combat buff.

Every Mr. Cabin Stamp Reward

Rewards are listed from left to right, starting from the top line.

Trainee Card

  • Watcher Bot Data / Sector Guard Bot Data / Overclocked Weaponry Mod
  • Skirmisher Mod / Walker Bot Data / Self Defense Response Mod
  • Crusher Bot Data / Red Gate Key / Hugh's Exolayer Outfit
  • Bingo: Cartdrigde Holder
  • Triple Bingo: Freeze Hacking Node
  • Blackout (Clearing the reward card): Diana's Lunar Aqua Outfit

Associate Card

  • Quick Fix Mod / Executor Bot Data / Secret Report 02 / Sweeper Bot
  • Spider Bot Data / Secret Report 01 / Reassembler Bot Data / Missile Watcher Bot Data
  • Hyperfocus Mod / Creator Bot Data / Performance Boost Mod / Red Gate Key
  • Secret Report 03 / Optimal Performance Mod / Hugh's Exolayer Type B Outfit / Cartridge Holder
  • Bingo: Expose Hacking Node
  • Triple Bingo: Drone Hive Defense Unit
  • Blackout (Clearing the reward car): Hugh's Legion Zero outfit

Which Rewards Should You Prioritize?

If you aren't planning to 100% the game, you should prioritize the following rewards:

  1. Any reward that unlocks new combat and build options - Mods and Cardridge Holders
  2. Red Gate Keys: Used to open Red Gate Zones, which reward you with unique rewards upon completing and unlock sealed sections of Stamp Cards
  3. Secret Reports, which add some backstory details
  4. Any cosmetic and collectible

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.

item.title: PRAGMATA: Mouse Trap Training Simulation Guide (Defeat All Enemies Within The Recycler)
item.content:encoded:
Among PRAGMATA's optional Training Simulations, there are some of them that can be difficult to complete while clearing all of the optional objectives. Besides the Medic Training Simulation, the Mouse Trap simulation can be challenging to clear fully, as you will need to be patient and make full use of the Decoy Gun.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Defeat All Enemies Within The Recyler + Complete Within 50 Seconds
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

NOTE: For any replay of any training mission, you can get 50 Lunafilament for clearing objectives you already cleared in a previous run.

Defeat All Enemies Within The Recyler + Complete Within 50 Seconds

While completing the Mouse Trap training mission by clearing enemies is relatively simple, clearing the Defeat all Enemies with the Recycler and Complete Within 50 secs objectives is trickier. The Recycler can only be used once, so you need to make absolutely sure every bot that spawns around the arena gets inside it before activating.

This can be achieved fairly easily by following the strategy below.

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. Collect the Decoy Gun
  2. Don't fire your Decoy just yet! Get in the middle of the recycler and wait about 10 seconds for the bots to reach you. You may have to dodge a few of their attacks. If you are getting damaged, it's not a big deal.
  3. After the ten seconds have passed, fire the Decoy gun to make sure the bots don't leave the Recycler.
  4. Activate it by completing the activation puzzle.
  5. This will instantly destroy every bot, clearing both optional objectives.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

item.title: PRAGMATA: Bad Blood Training Simulation Guide (Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire)
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA's Training Simulations, such as Medic Training, Mouse Trap, and Floor It, feel more like puzzles, forcing you to take full advantage of the game's mechanics to clear them with all optional objectives.

This is also the case of the Bad Blood Training Simulation, where you need to use hacking mechanics to your advantage to perfect it.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire + Complete Within 100 Seconds
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire + Complete Within 100 Seconds

To clear these objectives, you need to leverage the synergy between the Multihack and Confuse hacking nodes. Any time you go through a Multihack node, every other effect activated during that hacking session will apply to any nearby enemy, including Confusion. This effectively lets you pit multiple bots against one another.

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. At the start of the simulation, wait for every enemy to spawn while you acquire both Multihack and Confuse.
  2. Target the Impeder, and hack it by passing through a Multihack and a Confuse node. This will make every enemy attack one another.
  3. Don't wait for them to destroy one another, however. Attack a few of them, otherwise you will not be able to clear both waves of bots within 100 seconds.
  4. Repeat the same when the second wave spawns. Hack to open enemies and attack, but don't waste multihack or confuse if you don't need to activate the effects again - you only have four total uses.
  5. Keep damaging the weaker bots until they are at critical health. This will make it easier for the more powerful bots to dispose of them to clear the Defeat 4 enemies with friendly fire.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

item.title: PRAGMATA: Floor It Training Simulation Guide (Complete Without Taking Damage)
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA Floor It Training Simulation is a tricky simulation, much more so than the already challenging Medic Training and Mouse Trap simulations. While it is labeled as a combat training simulation, it is also a traversal challenge as you need to disengage locks and quickly alternate between jumping and shooting to complete all three objectives.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Complete Without Taking Damage
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

Complete Without Taking Damage

The most difficult objective for this training simulation is to complete it without taking damage. The Grapple Gun cannot damage bots, so you will need to rely on the Stasis Net Unit to prevent the flying bots from shooting at you and to stop the Executor in the middle section as you unlock the door via hacking.

To clear this objective, follow the strategy below

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. Collect the Stasis Net at the start of the simulation.
  2. Shoot at the group of flying bots using the Stasis Net. This will also destroy the first storage box.
  3. Get on the platform and wait for it to move. Traverse to the second area.
  4. Here you will find two rotating laser hazards, an Executor and a locked door.
  5. Jump, and while in the air, hold L2/LT to start unlocking the first lock. Time will slow down while you are in the air, giving you more time to complete the sequence.
  6. Shoot the Stasis Net at the Executor to stop it. Jump and work on the second lock. Remember to destroy the storage box sitting between the two laser traps.
  7. Get through the door, pick the Stasis Net, and wait for the lasers to your right to appear.
  8. Follow them and shoot at the flying bot as you get closer with the Stasis Net. This will also destroy the storage box.
  9. This is the trickiest part. Jump on the first step of the massive stairs and shoot the Stasis Net at the two flying bots to your right.
  10. Jump to the second step and start aiming at the second group on the left. Once the Stasis Net has reloaded, shoot immediately.
  11. Rush towards the goal. When you see the platform with the final storage box, approach the goal, shoot at it to destroy the storage box.
  12. Get to the goal to complete the Training Simulation with all optional objectives.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. item.title: PRAGMATA: Why You Should Always Finish Bots With a Critical Shot
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA features a few useful abilities with clear uses, and some whose benefits are not apparent. This is the case of Critical Shot, an early-game ability that can make your life much easier in some unexpected ways.

Table of contents

  • The Benefits of Finishing Enemies With a Critical Shot
  • How to Unlock Critical Shot
    • Using Critical Shot, Step-by-Step

The Benefits of Finishing Enemies With a Critical Shot

Finishing enemies with a Critical Shot, even without having it upgraded at level 3 or without having already collected certain mods that enhance the ability, is always worth it, for two reasons:

  • Every bot defeated with a Critical Shot drops extra Lunafilament. This makes it easier and faster to upgrade every weapon, ability, and hacking node to its maximum level.
  • During the animation, you are fully invincible, allowing you to ease off the pressure from enemies if you are surrounded by them.

How to Unlock Critical Shot

Critical Shot can be unlocked after completing Sector 01: Solar Power Plant. Access the Unit Printer in the Shelter, navigate to the Abilities Tab, and you will find the ability. It requires 1 Pure Lunum to unlock.

Using Critical Shot, Step-by-Step

  1. Damage the enemy until it is in critical health to make the Critical Shot hacking node appear on the Hacking Matrix.
  2. Navigate the Hacking Matrix and go through the node, successfully completing the hack to knock the enemy down.
  3. Perform the finishing blow by pressing the R2/RT/ZR button while the enemy is open.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack.
